name: kagome_3x3_e0_sector_spectrum

method.lanczos: memory-lean projecting Lanczos (3-term recurrence + in-loop p6m projection, no Krylov-basis storage)
method.rng_seed_strategy: deterministic xorshift seed → projection into sector → normalise

sectors.comment: Hit max_iters before Ritz-value tol; reported value is upper bound on true E_0(B_2). True value -11.4338926663 (libirrep ED).

global_ground_state.comment: kagome 3×3 PBC AFM Heisenberg ground state lives in the (Γ, A_1) sector — the trivial irrep of C_6v.  This DIFFERS from the L=2 cluster where the GS lives in B_1.  Pre-existing project documentation (research_kagome_irrep_scan.c header) had B_1 listed as the L=3 GS — that statement was incorrect; corrected in this commit.

spin_gap.definition: E_1 − E_0 across sectors; A_1 → A_2/B_1 (degenerate)
spin_gap.comment: A_2 and B_1 are degenerate to 4e-10 — exact symmetry-related multiplet pair on this cluster.  libirrep ED gives the same gap to ~1e-9 precision.

validation.libirrep_ED_agreement: all four sector ground states match libirrep_sg_heisenberg_sector_build_at_k to ~1-3 × 10⁻⁹

performance.vs_libirrep_ED_runtime: libirrep ED at L=3 finishes in 159 s for all 4 sectors — ~75× faster than ours.  But: libirrep builds the sector-restricted sparse Hamiltonian (~186K states per sector) directly; we work in the full 134M-state Hilbert space and project at every Krylov step.  Trade-off: our pipeline is uniform across sectors and lattice geometries; libirrep needs sector-specific sparse-matrix construction logic per Hamiltonian.

notes: First systematic sector-resolved spectrum at N=27 PBC kagome via the project's own infrastructure, validated against libirrep sector ED at machine precision.  Memory-lean projecting Lanczos (commit 7ca44c3) + 3-bit-chunk projector optimisation (dbca378) make this practical on a single-node M2 Ultra (~3.3 hours wall, ~4 GB working set).  Corrects pre-existing documentation in research_kagome_irrep_scan.c that claimed B_1 GS at L=3.
